content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

John H. Larson
Chairman

(916) 322-5901

June 6, 1988


Michael B. Montgomery
Redevelopment Agency Counsel
City of Huntington Park
c/o 215 North Marengo Avenue
Second Floor
Pasadena, California 91101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-192

Dear Commissioner Montgomery:

You have written on behalf of Huntington Park Redevelopment
Agency Board Member Jim Roberts. You have requested that we
provide informal assistance with regard to possible issues
raised under the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1/

QUESTION

May a redevelopment agency board member become employed by
a developer who does business in the redevelopment agency area
where employment will relate to projects in other
jurisdictions?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit public officials from engaging in
private employment. The Act does prohibit public officials
from making, participating in, or using their official
positions to influence governmental decisions affecting a

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations. Informal assistance does not provide the
requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of
Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 * P.O. Box 507 * Sacramento CA 95504-0807

Michael B. Montgomery
June 6, 1988
Page -2-

private source of income. If the developer has future business
with the redevelopment agency, disqualification would likely be
required.2/

FACTS

You are counsel for the Huntington Park redevelopment
agency. In that capacity, you have sought our assistance in
responding to a question posed by one of your clients, Mr. Jim
Roberts is a councilmember and redevelopment agency board
member in the City of Huntington Park.

Mr. Roberts is considering possible employment with a
developer who has in the past done work in the redevelopment
agency project area. The developer currently has a disposition
and development agreement with the redevelopment agency, which
was entered into by the agency director. Mr. Roberts had no
involvement in that agreement as an agency board member and has
no knowledge of the details of the agreement.

The developer wishes to undertake redevelopment agency
assisted shopping center projects in other communities, outside
Huntington Park. Mr. Roberts is considering going to work for
the developer on a commission basis for the purpose of seeking
development opportunities in these other jurisdictions.
However, in the future, the developer may have other dealings
with either the City of Huntington Park or its redevelopment
agency.

ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits a public official from making,
participating in, or using his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know
he has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) Mr. Roberts is
a public official in both his capacity as a councilmember and
as a redevelopment agency board member. (Section 82048.)

A public official has a financial interest in a decision if
the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable material
financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally, on the official, a member of the official's
immediate family, or on:

_____________________
2/ If the developer would be entering into a contract with
either the city or the redevelopment agency, the provisions of
Government Code Section 1090 should be considered.

Michael B. Montgomery
June 6, 1988
Page -3-


. . . (c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250)
or more in value provided to, received by or promised
to the public official within 12 months prior to the
time when the decision is made.

Section 87103

Consequently, if Mr. Roberts receives or is promised $250
in income by the developer, he will be required to disqualify
himself for a period of one year from any participation in a
governmental decision affecting the developer. This would
include any direct dealings with the developer by either the
city or the redevelopment agency. (Regulation 18702.1.)

However, the Act does not restrict Mr. Roberts private
economic activities. The Act only affects his role in his
official capacity with the city and the redevelopment agency.
Consequently, the Act would not prohibit Mr. Roberts from
undertaking such employment. As a caveat, you and Mr. Roberts
may wish to consider the possible implications of his situation
under Government Code Section 1090, et seq. As you are aware,
the Commission does not have jurisdiction with regard to that
section and does not provide advice with respect to it.

In addition to the foregoing, Mr. Roberts should remember
that if disqualification is required, it applies to using his
official position to influence a governmental decision as well
as to direct involvement. Therefore, he could not contact city
or redevelopment agency staff relative to a decision involving
the developer once the developer is a source of income to him.

I trust that this letter responds to the question you have
posed. Should you or Mr. Roberts have questions about this
letter, I may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,


Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel


by: Robert E. Leiggh
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:REL:ld:88192

LAW OFFICES

MICHAEL B. MONTGOMERY
A LAW CORPORATION

                                                    MAY 21 | 15 PM 38

                                                    215 NORTH MARENGO AVENUE
                                                    SECOND FLOOR
                                                    PASADENA, CALIFORNIA 91101
                                                    TELEPHONE (818) 405-9191

May 18, 1988
                                                    ALSO ADMITTED TO FLORIDA
                                                    AND HAWAII STATE BARS




Diane Griffiths
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Diane:

    Enclosed please find a letter from one of my Councilmen/Redevelopment Agency Members in Huntington Park.  The facts behind the request are as follows:

    1.    The Councilmember is quite involved politically and is currently president of the Independent Cities Association; his contacts and friendships are many.

    2.    There is a local major real estate developer in Huntington Park who wishes to branch out to other cities/redevelopment agencies and do agency assisted shopping centers, etc.

    3.    The Councilman would go to work on a commission basis for the developer seeking development opportunities in cities other than Huntington Park.

I see nothing wrong with this situation legally, so long as the council member abstains from any deliberations, staff contacts, or voting with respect to any interests of the developer within the City of Huntington Park or its redevelopment agency.  However, because of my position as a Commissioner, I wish to have back-up for obvious reasons since I am compensated by the Board upon which the requesting member sits.  Either a new advice letter or reference to existing advice letters requested.

                                                    Very truly yours,


                                                    MICHAEL B. MONTGOMERY

MBM/JI
Enclosure
cc: Councilman Roberts

City of
HUNTINGTON PARK california
CIVIC CENTER HUNTINGTON PARK 90255 • (213) 582-6161

MEMBERS OF CITY COUNCIL
THOMAS E. JACKSON
MAYOR

JACK W. PARKS       HERB R. HENNES. JR       JIM ROBERTS
WILLIAM P CUNNINGHAM


Apr il 19, 1988


Michael Montgomery
215 N. Marengo Avenue
2nd Floor
Pasadena, CA 91101

Dear Mike:

     I request a ruling from the Agency Attorney regarding my
employment by a developer that has done business in the City of
Huntington Park. I have not accepted nor discussed any financial
arrangements at this time and before I do, I need to know if it is
legal to do so.

     The developer currently has one DDA given to him by the Di-
rector, but no action has been taken by the Agency and I am not
aware of the details of the DDA. What would be my status?

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Jim Roberts
                                        Councilman

JR:jw

                                        RECEIVED APR 2 i 1988
